summary: Matched the small menu/name-entry wrapper function mnNameNew_8023EA08 by adding the mnnamenew C/header stubs and implementing the function as a direct call to mnNameNew_EnterFromMnCharSel(arg0, 4). The PR also established placeholder declarations for nearby mnNameNew/name-entry functions so the matched wrapper could typecheck against a new mnnamenew.h.
what_changed: Created/populated src/melee/mn/mnnamenew.c with an include of mnnamenew.h, a list of documented decomp stub markers for nearby functions, and a matched implementation of mnNameNew_8023EA08.
Implemented mnNameNew_8023EA08(UNK_T arg0) as a simple wrapper that calls mnNameNew_EnterFromMnCharSel(arg0, 4).

Created/populated src/melee/mn/mnnamenew.h with an include guard MELEE_MN_NAME_NEW_H, placeholder.h include, address-commented prototypes for mnNameNew/name-entry functions, and a concrete prototype for mnNameNew_EnterFromMnCharSel(UNK_T, int).

Added placeholder UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations for many surrounding functions, including mnNameNew_8023B0F8, mnNameNew_KeySetup, PickAutoName, NameContainsOnlySpaces, WriteCharactersForNameAtIndex, AddCharacterToName, mnNameNew_GlyphVariantInput, mnNameNew_MainInput, InitNameEntryUIState, mnNameNew_EnterFromMnName, and others.
smart_moves: Reduced the target function to the obvious semantic wrapper instead of overcomplicating it: mnNameNew_8023EA08 forwards its argument and a literal mode/index value 4 to mnNameNew_EnterFromMnCharSel.
Introduced a local header for the mnnamenew translation unit before matching the wrapper, making the call target and nearby functions explicit rather than relying on implicit declarations.
Used address comments in the header and decomp stub markers in the C file to preserve the function ordering/map context for future incremental work on the same menu module.

Kept unknown types honest with UNK_T, UNK_RET, and UNK_PARAMS where exact signatures were not yet established, while giving the called function mnNameNew_EnterFromMnCharSel the minimum concrete signature needed for this match.
decomp_lessons: Very small wrapper functions are acceptable PR targets in this project; the reviewer explicitly stated that PR size does not matter and incomplete functions are okay.

When matching a tiny function that calls another not-yet-decompiled function, it can be enough to add a precise prototype for the callee while leaving surrounding unknown functions as placeholder prototypes.
For menu code, establishing the source/header scaffold and nearby symbol list can be useful groundwork even if only one function is matched in the PR.
Do not infer full signatures for the whole module just to match one function; preserve uncertainty with placeholders until more evidence is available.
naming_conventions: Module-prefixed unknown functions use the form mnNameNew_8023EA08, with the address suffix retained when no descriptive name is known.

Descriptive names are used where prior knowledge exists, such as PickAutoName, NameContainsOnlySpaces, WriteCharactersForNameAtIndex, AddCharacterToName, InitNameEntryUIState, mnNameNew_KeySetup, mnNameNew_GlyphVariantInput, and mnNameNew_EnterFromMnCharSel.
Local/static-style unknown callback names in this area appear with the fn_8023XXXX convention, such as fn_8023CFC8, fn_8023D0F8, fn_8023DAEC, and fn_8023DBE8.
Header include guard added for this module was MELEE_MN_NAME_NEW_H.
Address comments in prototypes omit the leading 80 and use forms like /* 23EA08 */ next to the symbol declaration.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched mnNameNew_8023EA08 as a tail/simple forwarding wrapper: void mnNameNew_8023EA08(UNK_T arg0) { mnNameNew_EnterFromMnCharSel(arg0, 4); }.
Used a concrete integer literal 4 as the second argument to mnNameNew_EnterFromMnCharSel, which is likely the key constant needed for byte-for-byte matching of this wrapper.
Added the callee prototype as void mnNameNew_EnterFromMnCharSel(UNK_T, int), avoiding default argument promotions or mismatched call codegen.
The PR referenced a decomp.me scratch pad at https://decomp.me/scratch/ZErEP, but the provided evidence does not include the scratch details.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Welcomed the new contributor and stated that small PRs are acceptable: PR size does not matter and the project will accept changes of any size in that regard.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Clarified that submitting incomplete functions is okay, then noted they saw how to match the submitted function and would make a commit.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS in src/melee/mn/mnnamenew.h with concrete signatures.
Search for additional matches in src/melee/mn/mnnamenew.c around mnNameNew_EnterFromMnCharSel and mnNameNew_EnterFromMnName.
Check whether the literal 4 passed by mnNameNew_8023EA08 corresponds to a named enum or menu entry mode in later menu/name-entry decomp work.
Look for subsequent naming refinements for mnNameNew_8023EA08 or surrounding address-suffixed mnNameNew functions.
